title: Contesting the far right: a psychoanalytic and feminist critical theory approach [Book Review]
author: Travis

opening_move.description: The review opens with the central question driving Leeb's book: why do citizens turn to hateful, racist politics that run against their own interests? This frames the book as a response to the explanatory gap in far-right studies.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Providing a multi-dimensional theoretical framework to understand far-right radicalization beyond either economic or pathological explanations

rivals_engaged: pathologising approaches to the far right
theoretical_positioning.description: The review is appreciative and expository, endorsing Leeb's synthesis of psychoanalysis, critical theory, and decolonial theory for understanding far-right politics.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: psychoanalytic and critical theory reading of far-right politics
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review; primarily expository. The review presents and contextualises Leeb's theoretical framework.

case_type.case_name: Leeb, Contesting the Far Right (2024); contemporary far-right politics

confidence_notes: Book review genre; largely expository and appreciative. Normative stake is medium because the review endorses Leeb's critical-diagnostic project without adding an independent argument.
